---
title: "Negative Binomial Modeling"
author: "Ian Sargent"
date: '`r Sys.Date()`'
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

### Packages
```{r}
# Load packages with pacman library
pacman::p_load(
  tidyverse, # Data manipulation
  sf, # Working with simple feature geometries
  janitor, # Column name cleaning
  MASS, # For negative binomial regression model
  fixest, # Fixed effects model
  randomForest, # For random forest feature selection
  vip, # Variable importance plot for random forest
  glmnet # LASSO regression / regularization (also feature selection)
)

citation("tidycensus")
```

### Read Data
```{r}
nb_data <- read_csv("https://raw.githubusercontent.com/iansargent/DC-Metro-Crime/refs/heads/main/Data/Combined/nb_data.csv", show_col_types = FALSE) |>
  dplyr::select(-c(year_f, month_f, line))
```


## Predicting Violent Crimes

### Feature Selection Methods

#### Determining Best Distance Metric by AIC
```{r}
# Just including continuous distance variable
# THIS IS OUR MAIN MODEL (for now)
nb_violent_continuous <- fixest::fenegbin(
  violent_crimes ~ dist_to_nearest_metro + above_ground + transfer_station +
    green_yellow_line + blue_orange_silver_line +
    median_household_income + pct_poverty + pct_unemployed +
    pct_black_nh + pct_renter + pct_vacant +
    pct_bachelors_only + offset(log_pop) |
    factor(year) + factor(month),
  cluster = ~tract,
  data = nb_data
) |> summary()

# Just including the buffers (400m and 800m)
nb_violent_buffers <- fixest::fenegbin(
  violent_crimes ~ stations_within_800m + stations_within_400m + above_ground + transfer_station +
    green_yellow_line + blue_orange_silver_line +
    median_household_income + pct_poverty + pct_unemployed +
    pct_black_nh + pct_renter + pct_vacant +
    pct_bachelors_only + offset(log_pop) |
    factor(year) + factor(month),
  cluster = ~tract,
  data = nb_data
)

# Including all distance variables included
nb_violent <- fixest::fenegbin(
  violent_crimes ~ stations_within_800m + stations_within_400m + dist_to_nearest_metro +
    above_ground + transfer_station +
    green_yellow_line + blue_orange_silver_line +
    median_household_income + pct_poverty + pct_unemployed +
    pct_black_nh + pct_renter + pct_vacant +
    pct_bachelors_only + offset(log_pop) |
    factor(year) + factor(month),
  cluster = ~tract,
  data = nb_data
)

# Compare AIC (Lower = Better!) -->
# RESULT: Just including the continuous distance variable had the lowest AIC
tibble(
  spec = c("Dist only", "Buffers only", "All three"),
  AIC = c(AIC(nb_violent), AIC(nb_violent_continuous), AIC(nb_violent_buffers)),
  n_metro_vars = c(1, 2, 3)
) |> arrange(AIC)
```


#### Random Forest Feature Selection
```{r}
# SOURCE (Random Forest Feature Selection): https://ieeexplore.ieee.org/document/8710733
# SOURCE: https://www.google.com/search?q=negative+binomial+feature+selection+random+forest&sca_esv=2249988ea6ef4f61&rlz=1C5CHFA_enUS1073US1073&sxsrf=ANbL-n7bvmICG1wBm9_UHwTAuE9A0HGRaw%3A1773712648315&ei=CLW4aYb1ErDV5NoPj6_zsQ8&biw=1440&bih=692&ved=0ahUKEwiGmdjC6qWTAxWwKlkFHY_XPPYQ4dUDCBE&uact=5&oq=negative+binomial+feature+selection+random+forest&gs_lp=Egxnd3Mtd2l6LXNlcnAiMW5lZ2F0aXZlIGJpbm9taWFsIGZlYXR1cmUgc2VsZWN0aW9uIHJhbmRvbSBmb3Jlc3QyBRAhGKABMgUQIRigATIFECEYoAEyBRAhGKABMgUQIRigATIFECEYqwJI3xdQnQFYqhZwAXgBkAEAmAHfAaABixSqAQYwLjExLjO4AQPIAQD4AQGYAg-gAskUwgIKEAAYRxjWBBiwA5gDAIgGAZAGCJIHBjEuMTEuM6AH4TyyBwYwLjExLjO4B8UUwgcGMS4xMi4yyAcfgAgB&sclient=gws-wiz-serp

# Set a random seed
set.seed(123)

# Select model variables and drop incomplete rows
rf_data_v <- nb_data |>
  dplyr::select(
    violent_crimes, dist_to_nearest_metro, above_ground, transfer_station,
    red_line, green_yellow_line, blue_orange_silver_line,
    median_household_income, pct_poverty, pct_unemployed,
    pct_black_nh, pct_hispanic, pct_renter, pct_vacant,
    pct_bachelors_only
  )

# Run the random forest predicting violent crimes (with 500 trees)
rf_violent <- randomForest(
  violent_crimes ~ .,
  data = rf_data_v,
  ntree = 500,
  importance = TRUE,
  na.action = na.roughfix # Impute NA values
)

# Create a variable importance barplot showing which variables were the most selected to predict violent crime counts
importance(rf_violent) |>
  as.data.frame() |>
  rownames_to_column("variable") |>
  arrange(desc(`%IncMSE`)) |>
  mutate(variable = fct_reorder(variable, `%IncMSE`)) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = `%IncMSE`, y = variable)) +
  geom_col(fill = "#2980b9") +
  labs(
    title    = "Random Forest Feature Importance — Violent Crime",
    subtitle = "% Increase in MSE when variable is permuted",
    x        = "% Increase in MSE",
    y        = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12)
```


#### LASSO Regression (Regularization)
```{r}
# Random Seed
set.seed(123)

lasso_df_v <- nb_data |>
  dplyr::select(
    -c(
      tract, year, month, nearest_station_id, property_crimes,
      nearest_station_id, line, total_crimes
    )
  ) |>
  drop_na()

# Build components

# Predictors
X_v <- lasso_df_v[, -1] |>
  as.matrix() |>
  scale()
# Response
y_v <- lasso_df_v$violent_crimes |> as.matrix()
# Offset
offset_v <- lasso_df_v$log_pop |> as.matrix()

# Violent crime LASSO regression with cross-validation
cv_lasso_v <- cv.glmnet(
  x      = X_v,
  y      = y_v,
  family = "poisson", # Using poisson family since no support for negative binomial
  alpha  = 1,
  offset = offset_v,
  nfolds = 10,
)

# Define the coefficients that were not shrunk to zero (ie. Important features to include)
surviving_v <- coef(cv_lasso_v, s = "lambda.min") |>
  as.matrix() |>
  as.data.frame() |>
  rownames_to_column("variable") |>
  rename(coefficient = s1) |>
  filter(coefficient != 0, variable != "(Intercept)") |>
  # Drop time FE rows for readability
  filter(!str_detect(variable, "factor")) |>
  arrange(desc(abs(coefficient)))

# Table showing the "surviving" variables
surviving_v |>
  knitr::kable(digits = 4, caption = "LASSO Surviving Variables — Violent Crime")
```


## Predicting Property Crimes

### Feature Selection

#### Best Distance Metric by AIC
```{r}
# Just including continuous distance variable
nb_property_continuous <- fixest::fenegbin(
  property_crimes ~ dist_to_nearest_metro + above_ground + transfer_station +
    green_yellow_line + blue_orange_silver_line +
    median_household_income + pct_poverty + pct_unemployed +
    pct_black_nh + pct_hispanic + pct_renter + pct_vacant +
    pct_bachelors_only + offset(log_pop) |
    factor(year) + factor(month),
  vcov = "hetero",
  data = nb_data
)

# Just including the buffer distance variables (400m and 800m)
nb_property_buffers <- fixest::fenegbin(
  property_crimes ~ stations_within_800m + stations_within_400m + above_ground + transfer_station +
    green_yellow_line + blue_orange_silver_line +
    median_household_income + pct_poverty + pct_unemployed +
    pct_black_nh + pct_hispanic + pct_renter + pct_vacant +
    pct_bachelors_only + offset(log_pop) |
    factor(year) + factor(month),
  vcov = "hetero",
  data = nb_data
)

# Including all distance variables
nb_property <- fixest::fenegbin(
  property_crimes ~ stations_within_800m + stations_within_400m + dist_to_nearest_metro +
    above_ground + transfer_station +
    green_yellow_line + blue_orange_silver_line +
    median_household_income + pct_poverty + pct_unemployed +
    pct_black_nh + pct_hispanic + pct_renter + pct_vacant +
    pct_bachelors_only + offset(log_pop) |
    factor(year) + factor(month),
  vcov = "hetero",
  data = nb_data
)

# Compare AIC (Lower = Better!)
# RESULT: Just including the continuous distance variable had the lowest AIC
tibble(
  spec = c("Dist only", "Buffers only", "All three"),
  AIC = c(AIC(nb_property), AIC(nb_property_continuous), AIC(nb_property_buffers)),
  n_metro_vars = c(1, 2, 3)
) |> arrange(AIC)
```


#### Random Forest Feature Importance
```{r}
rf_data_p <- nb_data |>
  dplyr::select(
    property_crimes, dist_to_nearest_metro, above_ground, transfer_station,
    red_line, green_yellow_line, blue_orange_silver_line,
    median_household_income, pct_poverty, pct_unemployed,
    pct_black_nh, pct_hispanic, pct_renter, pct_vacant,
    pct_bachelors_only
  )


rf_property <- randomForest(
  property_crimes ~ .,
  data = rf_data_p,
  ntree = 500,
  importance = TRUE,
  na.action = na.roughfix
)

importance(rf_property) |>
  as.data.frame() |>
  rownames_to_column("variable") |>
  arrange(desc(`%IncMSE`)) |>
  mutate(variable = fct_reorder(variable, `%IncMSE`)) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = `%IncMSE`, y = variable)) +
  geom_col(fill = "#2980b9") +
  labs(
    title    = "Random Forest Feature Importance — Property Crime",
    subtitle = "% Increase in MSE when variable is permuted",
    x        = "% Increase in MSE",
    y        = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12)
```


#### LASSO Regression

```{r}
lasso_df_p <- nb_data |>
  dplyr::select(
    -c(
      tract, year, month, nearest_station_id, violent_crimes,
      nearest_station_id, line, total_crimes
    )
  ) |>
  drop_na()

# Build components

# Predictors
X_p <- lasso_df_p[, -1] |>
  as.matrix() |>
  scale()
# Response
y_p <- lasso_df_p$property_crimes |> as.matrix()
# Offset
offset_p <- lasso_df_p$log_pop |> as.matrix()

# Property crime LASSO
cv_lasso_p <- cv.glmnet(
  x      = X_p,
  y      = y_p,
  family = "poisson",
  alpha  = 1,
  offset = offset_p,
  nfolds = 10
)

surviving_p <- coef(cv_lasso_p, s = "lambda.min") |>
  as.matrix() |>
  as.data.frame() |>
  rownames_to_column("variable") |>
  rename(coefficient = s1) |>
  filter(coefficient != 0, variable != "(Intercept)") |>
  filter(!str_detect(variable, "factor")) |>
  arrange(desc(abs(coefficient)))

surviving_p |>
  knitr::kable(digits = 4, caption = "LASSO Surviving Variables — Property Crime")
```


## Model Evaluation

### Rootograms
```{r}
rootogram_data <- function(model, outcome, max_count = 30) {
  # Observed frequencies
  obs <- table(factor(outcome, levels = 0:max_count))
  counts <- as.numeric(names(obs))

  # Predicted frequencies using NB distribution
  mu <- fitted(model)
  theta <- model$theta

  expected <- sapply(counts, function(k) {
    sum(dnbinom(k, size = theta, mu = mu))
  })

  tibble(
    count    = counts,
    observed = as.numeric(obs),
    expected = expected,
    sqrt_obs = sqrt(as.numeric(obs)),
    sqrt_exp = sqrt(expected),
    diff     = sqrt_obs - sqrt_exp # hanging bar height
  )
}

# Build rootogram data
root_v <- rootogram_data(nb_violent_continuous, nb_data$violent_crimes)
root_p <- rootogram_data(nb_property_continuous, nb_data$property_crimes)

# Plot function
plot_rootogram <- function(root_df, title) {
  ggplot(root_df, aes(x = count)) +
    # Hanging bars — drop from sqrt(expected) down by diff
    geom_rect(aes(
      xmin = count - 0.4,
      xmax = count + 0.4,
      ymin = sqrt_exp - sqrt_obs,
      ymax = sqrt_exp
    ), fill = "steelblue", color = "white") +
    # Expected curve
    geom_line(aes(y = sqrt_exp), color = "red", linewidth = 0.8) +
    geom_point(aes(y = sqrt_exp), color = "red", size = 1.5) +
    # Zero reference line
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "solid", color = "black") +
    labs(
      title    = title,
      subtitle = "Hanging rootogram — bars above 0 = underfitting, below 0 = overfitting",
      x        = "Crime Count",
      y        = "√ Frequency"
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 12)
}

plot_rootogram(root_v, "Violent Crimes: Fitted vs Actual Counts")
plot_rootogram(root_p, "Property Crimes: Fitted vs Actual Counts")
```
